title: Test Strategy, Contracts, Properties, and Mutation · Broken-Build Bay
summary: Design a layered test suite around risks rather than file counts. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a risk-to-test coverage matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Assign unit, integration, contract, browser, system, and acceptance tests to distinct risks.
Test public behavior, invariants, boundaries, and failure recovery.
Use generated cases for algebraic properties, parser boundaries, and state invariants.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Flaky Tests evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Flaky Tests assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Detect, quarantine briefly, diagnose, and remove nondeterminism with ownership. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates tests. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Flaky Tests, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a risk-to-test coverage matrix.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Retrying a flaky test converts it into a reliable gate. This fails the Flaky Tests contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Biome Linter Rules (Biome 2.5) — Controls modern JavaScript and TypeScript formatting, correctness, accessibility, and unused-code lint examples.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect, quarantine briefly, diagnose, and remove nondeterminism with ownership.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a gates-flaky-tests-y4gzjd: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Flaky Tests into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-flaky-tests-y4gzjd:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: Name the Flaky Tests precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Flaky Tests applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Detect, quarantine briefly, diagnose, and remove nondeterminism with ownership.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Test Layers to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Test Layers.
